mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at the zero lower bound reduces the risk-free discount rate and compresses equity risk premiums, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 0.18% as of March 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 8.0% signals acute household balance sheet stress and collapsing consumption, which forces downward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rate at 8.5% as of March 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline of real retail sales and industrial production captures a simultaneous collapse in final demand and manufacturing output; their combined momentum predicts further negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings estimates continue to fall.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive when both RSAFS and INDPRO are declining, occurring in more than 50% of such joint-decline observations.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals that manufacturing capacity utilization is rebounding from depressed levels, which leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as operating leverage drives earnings beats.
failure_condition: Following month-over-month INDPRO increases in the 12 months after a recession trough, 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP indicates contracting aggregate demand, which reduces nominal corporate revenue growth and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors update recession probabilities.
failure_condition: Quarters with declining GDPC1 are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index reduces real corporate earnings expectations by compressing profit margins and lowering consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Headline CPI at 212.714 as of March 2009
failure_condition: Months with rising CPIAUCSL are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment signals falling aggregate income expenditure and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the labor market deterioration crosses a critical information threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 133019k as of March 2009
failure_condition: Months with declining PAYEMS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts above 600 thousand units signals a bottoming in credit-sensitive durable demand and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the housing multiplier revives upstream industries.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 583k as of February 2009
failure_condition: Months where HOUST exceeds 600 thousand are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
